title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg, drafted first overall by the Mavericks, expressed commitment to Dallas despite New England roots.
Flagg played his first NBA game at TD Garden, recalling childhood visits as a Celtics fan.
Flagg, averaging 20.3 points and 6.5 rebounds, is under contract with the Mavericks through 2029.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics lineup after 42 weeks, playing 27 minutes against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
Tatum achieved 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists, helping the Celtics win with a 25-point lead.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's effort and mentality, likening his return to adding a new player midseason.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to play after a 10-month recovery from a torn Achilles, starting with no minutes restriction.
Tatum scored 15 points with 12 rebounds and 7 assists, showing progress despite some rust during the Celtics' 120-100 win over the Mavericks.
Nikola Vucevic will miss a month due to a fractured ring finger; Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 24 points.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins chose not to make major trades, prioritizing long-term rebuilding over short-term gains at the trade deadline.
Bruins traded minor league players for prospects, avoiding costly deals in a competitive seller's market.
Boston holds four first-round picks over two years, focusing on future talent rather than immediate rentals.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby is set to join the Baltimore Ravens in exchange for two first-round draft picks from the Raiders.
Crosby, a five-time Pro Bowler, reportedly had interest in joining the New England Patriots and Dallas Cowboys.
Despite his interest, the Patriots likely couldn't match the Ravens' offer with their No. 31 draft pick this year.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic is out for a month with a fractured right ring finger sustained against Dallas.
Vucevic, who joined the Celtics at the trade deadline, averages 11.4 points and 8.8 rebounds per game.
His absence strains the Celtics' front-court depth, with limited backup options available for their upcoming games.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to play for the Boston Celtics against the Dallas Mavericks after a 297-day recovery from a ruptured Achilles.
Tatum received a thunderous ovation at TD Garden and played six minutes, recording two assists and two rebounds initially.
The Celtics are in second place in the Eastern Conference with a 41-21 record, defying expectations amidst Tatum's absence.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots are trading center Garrett Bradbury to the Chicago Bears for a fifth-round draft pick.
Bradbury started every game for the Patriots in 2025, contributing to their AFC Championship win.
The trade will clear $5.7 million in salary cap space for New England, applicable to 2026 free agency.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hamnet', a fictionalized account of Shakespeare's son, streams on Peacock; stars Jessie Buckley and Paul Mescal.
Iranian director Jafar Panahi's 'It Was Just an Accident' is available on Hulu, critiquing authoritarianism.
'Vladimir', starring Rachel Weisz and John Slattery, streams on Netflix, exploring college infidelity and scandals.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to the Celtics starting lineup for the first time this season against the Mavericks.
Coach Joe Mazzulla praises Tatum's journey and emphasizes teamwork as he rejoins after ten months away.
Tatum replaces Baylor Scheierman in the lineup with no announced minutes restriction for his first game back.
